content:
a

Day at Picnic

Rondiha Barrage

After a long and tiring bus journey to Bardhaman, our adventure at Rondiha Barrage was a much-needed respite. As we disembarked from the bus, the sun was shining brilliantly, and the warmth enveloped me, prompting me to shed my jacket. It was a welcoming change from the chilly bus ride.

The left side above photo was taken at the end of our picnic, but it's not just a simple snapshot. This image captures more than just a moment in time; it encapsulates the memories, the laughter, and the camaraderie that defined that special day.

We decided to explore the other side of the river, and to get there, we had to take a small boat. The boat ride was an exciting start to our day at Rondiha Barrage. As we gently glided across the water, I marveled at the vastness of the river, its surface glistening in the sunlight. On the opposite bank, we found a long stretch of sandy terrain where others were engrossed in a spirited game of volleyball. I, on the other hand, opted for a leisurely walk along the river's edge. The sand was soft beneath my feet, and the cool breeze carried the soothing sounds of flowing water. The boat ride back to the main picnic area was equally enjoyable, as we watched the Barrage loom closer, a feat of engineering against the backdrop of natural beauty. It was a day filled with contrasts – from the bustling bus journey to the serene riverbanks and the joyous camaraderie of the picnic-goers. Our visit to Rondiha Barrage was a memorable adventure that brought us closer to nature and each other.

In that moment captured in the photo, I found myself perched on a weathered rock by the side of the mighty Damodar River. The view was breathtaking as I faced the river, its turbulent waters flowing relentlessly downstream. The photo, taken from behind, showcased my silhouette against the backdrop of the river and the towering Damodar Barrage.

The fact that all the images from that memorable day were taken using Tapan's phone adds a unique and personal touch to the experience. Sometimes, it's not about having the latest or most advanced camera; it's about capturing the moment and the emotions attached to it.

One of the most captivating moments I captured during my visit was a photograph that seemed almost surreal. As the sun began its descent, casting a warm, golden glow across the landscape, I noticed a solitary figure approaching in the distance. It was a monk dressed in attire that resembled Lord Shiva, a deity known for his tranquil and divine presence.

What made this image truly magical was the vibrant bush of flowers that surrounded the base of the date palm. These flowers, bursting with hues of red, yellow, and pink, created a vivid contrast against the warm, golden tones of the setting sun. The flowers seemed to thrive in the tree's protective embrace, as if they were nurtured by its presence.
